volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 3.2.723c - Section 3.2.795)

sections.label: Section 3.2.723c
sections.original: Scis iam quíd loquar . síc decet .
sections.translation: The way that you yourselves proceed . But , Tranio , to say the truth , according as men are , it so befits you to humour them ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.725
sections.original: víta quam sít brevis , ⋯ simul cógita .
sections.translation: reflecting , at the same time , how short life is .

sections.label: Section 3.2.726
sections.translation: What of all this ? Dear me , at last , after some difficulty , I perceive that you are talking about these goings-on of ours .

sections.label: Section 3.2.728
sections.original: músice hercle ágitis aetátem , ita ut vós decet ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you people are living a merry life , just as befits you :

sections.label: Section 3.2.729_730
sections.original: vino et victu probo , piscatu electili
sections.translation: on wine , good cheer , nice dainty fish , you enjoy life .

sections.label: Section 3.2.731b
sections.translation: Why yes , so it was in time past , indeed ; but now these things have come to an end all at once .

sections.label: Section 3.2.733
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.733b
sections.translation: So utterly , Simo , are we all undone !

sections.label: Section 3.2.734
sections.original: Non taces ? prospere vobis cuncta usque adhuc
sections.translation: Won ’ t you hold your tongue ? Everything has gone on prosperously with you hitherto .

sections.label: Section 3.2.735b
sections.original: Ita ut dicis facta hau nego .
sections.translation: I don ’ t deny that it has been as you say ; undoubtedly , we have lived heartily , just as we pleased ; but , Simo , in such a way has the breeze now forsaken our ship —

sections.label: Section 3.2.737b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? In what way ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.738b
sections.translation: In a most shocking way .

sections.label: Section 3.2.738c
sections.translation: What , wasn ’ t it hauled ashore [*] in safety ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.739b
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 3.2.739c
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.739d
sections.translation: Ah wretched me ! I ’ m utterly undone !

sections.label: Section 3.2.740
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.740b
sections.original: Quia venit navis , nostrae navi quae frangat † ratem .
sections.translation: Because a ship has come , to smash the hull of our ship .

sections.label: Section 3.2.741
sections.original: Vellem út tu velles , Tranio . sed quid est negoti ?
sections.translation: I would wish as you would wish , Tranio , for your own sake . But what is the matter ? Do inform me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.741b
sections.translation: I will inform you . My master has arrived from abroad .

sections.label: Section 3.2.742b
sections.translation: In that case , the cord will be stretched for you ; thence to the place where iron fetters clink ; after that , straight to the cross .

sections.label: Section 3.2.743b
sections.original: Per tua te g enua obsecro ,
sections.translation: Now , by your knees , I do implore you , don ’ t give information to my master .

sections.label: Section 3.2.744_745b
sections.original: É me , ne quid metuas , nil sciet .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you fear ; he shall know nothing from me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.746
sections.translation: Blessings on you , my patron .

sections.label: Section 3.2.746b
sections.original: Nil moror mi istius modi clientis .
sections.translation: I don ’ t care for clients of this description for myself .

sections.label: Section 3.2.747
sections.original: Nunc hoc quod ad te noster me misit senex —
sections.translation: Now as to this about which our old gentleman has sent me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.748
sections.original: Hoc mihi responde primum , quod ego te rogo :
sections.translation: First answer me this that I ask you . As yet , has your old gentleman discovered anything of these matters ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.750
sections.translation: Nothing whatever .

sections.label: Section 3.2.750b
sections.translation: Has he censured his son at all ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.751
sections.original: Tam liquidust quam liquida esse tempestas solet ⋯
sections.translation: He is as calm as the calm weather is wont to be . Now he has requested me most earnestly to beg this of you , that leave may be given him to see over this house of yours .

sections.label: Section 3.2.754
sections.translation: It ’ s not for sale .

sections.label: Section 3.2.754b
sections.translation: I know that indeed ; but the old gentleman

sections.label: Section 3.2.755
sections.original: gynaeceum aedificare volt hic in suis
sections.translation: wishes to build a woman ’ s apartment [*] here in his own house , baths , too , and a piazza , and a porch .

sections.label: Section 3.2.757
sections.translation: What has he been dreaming of ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.757b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . He wishes to give his son a wife as soon as he can ; for that purpose he wants a new apartment for the women .

sections.label: Section 3.2.760
sections.original: nam sibi laudavisse hasce ait architectonem
sections.translation: But he says that some builder , I don ’ t know who , has been praising up to him this house of yours , as being remarkably well built ; now he ’ s desirous to take a model from it , if you don ’ t make any objection —

sections.label: Section 3.2.763
sections.original: nam ille eo maiore hinc opere ex te exemplum petit ,
sections.translation: Indeed , he is really choosing a plan for himself from a piece of poor workmanship .

sections.label: Section 3.2.764
sections.original: quia isti úmbram aestate tíbi esse audivit perbonam
sections.translation: It was because he heard that here the summer heat was much modified ; that this house was wont to be inhabited each day all day long .

sections.label: Section 3.2.766
sections.original: Immo edepol vero , quom usquequaque umbra est , tamen
sections.translation: Why really , upon my faith , on the contrary , while there ’ s shade in every direction , in spite of it , the sun is always here from morning till night : he stands , like a dun , continually at the door ; and I have no shade anywhere , unless , perhaps , there may be some in the well .

sections.label: Section 3.2.770
sections.original: Quid , Sarsinatis ecqua est , si Vmbram non habes ?
sections.translation: Well now , have you one from Sarsina , if you have no woman of Umbria [*] ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.771
sections.original: Molestus ne sis . haec sunt sicut praedico .
sections.translation: Don ’ t be impertinent . It is just as I tell you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.772
sections.translation: Still , he wishes to look over it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.772b
sections.translation: He may look over it , if he likes . If there is anything that takes his fancy , let him build after my plan .

sections.label: Section 3.2.774b
sections.translation: Am I to go and call this person hither ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.774c
sections.translation: Go and call him .

sections.label: Section 3.2.775
sections.original: Alexándrum magnum atque Agathoclem aiunt maximas
sections.translation: They say that Alexander the Great and Agathocles [*] achieved two very great exploits ; what shall be the lot of myself , a third , who , unaided , am achieving deeds imperishable ? This old fellow is carrying his pack-saddle , the other one , as well . I ’ ve hit upon a novel trade for myself , not a bad one ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.780
sections.translation: whereas muleteers have mules to carry pack-saddles ; I ’ ve got men to carry the pack-saddles . They are able to carry heavy burdens ; whatever you put upon them , they carry . Now , I don ’ t know whether I am to address him . I ’ ll accost him , however . ( Calling aloud .) Hark you , Theuropides !

sections.label: Section 3.2.784
sections.translation: Well ; who ’ s calling me ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.784b
sections.translation: A servant most attached to his master .

sections.label: Section 3.2.786
sections.original: Quod me miseras , adfero omne impetratum .
sections.translation: Where you sent me , I got it all agreed to .

sections.label: Section 3.2.787
sections.original: Quid illíc , opsecro , tam diu destitisti ?
sections.translation: Prithee , why did you stay there so long ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.788
sections.original: Seni non erat otium , id sum opperitus .
sections.translation: The old gentleman hadn ’ t leisure ; I was waiting until then .

sections.label: Section 3.2.789
sections.original: Antiquom optines hoc tuom , tardus ut sis .
sections.translation: You keep up that old way of yours , of being tardy .

sections.label: Section 3.2.790
sections.original: Heus tu , si voles verbum hóc cogitare ,
sections.translation: Hark you ! if you please reflect upon this proverb : to blow and swallow [*] at the same moment isn ’ t easy to be done ; I couldn ’ t be here and there at the same time .

sections.label: Section 3.2.793
sections.translation: What now ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.793b
sections.translation: Come and look , and inspect it at your own pleasure .

sections.label: Section 3.2.794
sections.translation: Very well , you go before me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.794b
sections.translation: Am I delaying to do so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.794c
sections.translation: I ’ ll follow after you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.795
sections.original: Senex ipsus te ante ostium eccum opperitur .
sections.translation: Look , the old gentleman himself is awaiting you before the door , but he is concerned that he has sold this house .
